prompt: Implement minigit as described in SPEC-v1.txt using Lua. The executable must be named 'minigit' and be runnable as ./minigit. For compiled languages, include a Makefile or build script. For interpreted languages, ensure the minigit file has a proper shebang line and is executable. Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v1.sh
response_text:
Certainly! Here’s a solid implementation of **minigit** in Lua, following the general requirements expected from such assignments (supporting `init`, `add`, `commit`, `status`, `log`, `checkout`, etc., similar to a minimal git).  
Make sure `SPEC-v1.txt` refers to these typical operations; if not, clarify the spec or paste it here!

We'll assume the standard features and interface.  
Lua is an interpreted language, so you'll have an executable file with a shebang.

---

## `minigit` (Place this in a file named `minigit`)

```lua
#!/usr/bin/env lua

-- Simple MiniGit implementation in Lua
-- Supports: init, add, commit, status, log, checkout

local lfs = require("lfs")

local MINIGIT_DIR = ".minigit"
local OBJECTS_DIR = MINIGIT_DIR .. "/objects"
local INDEX_FILE = MINIGIT_DIR .. "/index"
local HEAD_FILE = MINIGIT_DIR .. "/HEAD"
local LOG_FILE = MINIGIT_DIR .. "/log"

local function fatal(msg) io.stderr:write(msg..'\n') os.exit(1) end
local function file_exists(name) local f=io.open(name,"r") if f then f:close() return true else return false end end
local function mkdir(dir) if not lfs.mkdir(dir) then fatal("Cannot create directory: "..dir) end end

local function init()
    if file_exists(MINIGIT_DIR) then
        fatal("Repository already initialized.")
    end
    mkdir(MINIGIT_DIR)
    mkdir(OBJECTS_DIR)
    local f = io.open(INDEX_FILE, "w") f:close()
    f = io.open(HEAD_FILE, "w") f:close()
    f = io.open(LOG_FILE, "w") f:close()
    print("Initialized empty minigit repository in " .. lfs.currentdir() .. "/" .. MINIGIT_DIR)
end

local function read_index()
    local index = {}
    if not file_exists(INDEX_FILE) then return index end
    for line in io.lines(INDEX_FILE) do
        index[line] = true
    end
    return index
end

local function write_index(index)
    local f = io.open(INDEX_FILE, "w")
    for fname in pairs(index) do
        f:write(fname.."\n")
    end
    f:close()
end

local function sha1_file(filename)
    local f = io.open(filename, "rb") if not f then return nil end
    local content = f:read("*all") f:close()
    local tmpfile = os.tmpname()
    local tf = io.open(tmpfile, "wb") tf:write(content) tf:close()
    local h = io.popen("sha1sum "..tmpfile)
    local line = h:read("*l") h:close()
    os.remove(tmpfile)
    if not line then return nil end
    return line:match("^(%w+)")
end

local function add(filenames)
    local index = read_index()
    for _, fname in ipairs(filenames) do
        if not file_exists(fname) then
            fatal("File does not exist: "..fname)
        end
        index[fname] = true
    end
    write_index(index)
end

local function read_head()
    if not file_exists(HEAD_FILE) then return nil end
    for line in io.lines(HEAD_FILE) do
        return line
    end
    return nil
end

local function commit(msg)
    local index = read_index()
    if not next(index) then
        fatal("Nothing to commit.")
    end
    local tree = {}
    for fname in pairs(index) do
        local sha1 = sha1_file(fname)
        if not sha1 then fatal("Cannot read file: "..fname) end
        tree[fname] = sha1
        if not file_exists(OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha1) then
            -- store the blob
            local out = io.open(OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha1, "wb")
            local inF = io.open(fname, "rb")
            out:write(inF:read("*all"))
            out:close()
            inF:close()
        end
    end
    -- Store tree object (simple JSON-ish format)
    local tree_obj = ""
    for fname, sha1 in pairs(tree) do
        tree_obj = tree_obj..fname..":"..sha1.."\n"
    end
    local tree_sha1 = sha1_file(OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha1_file(tostring(os.time())))
    local treefile = OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..tree_sha1
    local tf = io.open(treefile, "w")
    tf:write(tree_obj)
    tf:close()

    -- New commit object
    local parent = read_head() or ""
    local commit_obj = {
        tree = tree_sha1,
        parent = parent,
        msg = msg,
        time = tostring(os.time())
    }

    local commit_data = ""
    for k,v in pairs(commit_obj) do
        commit_data = commit_data..k..":"..v.."\n"
    end
    local commit_sha1 = sha1_file(OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha1_file(tostring(os.time())..msg))
    local commitfile = OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..commit_sha1
    local cf = io.open(commitfile, "w") cf:write(commit_data) cf:close()

    local headf = io.open(HEAD_FILE, "w") headf:write(commit_sha1) headf:close()
    local logf = io.open(LOG_FILE, "a")
    logf:write(commit_sha1.." "..os.date("%c").." "..msg.."\n")
    logf:close()

    print("Committed as "..commit_sha1)
end

local function status()
    local index = read_index()
    if not next(index) then print("No files staged.") return end
    print("Staged files:")
    for fname in pairs(index) do
        print("  "..fname)
    end
end

local function log_()
    if not file_exists(LOG_FILE) then print("No commits yet.") return end
    for line in io.lines(LOG_FILE) do
        print(line)
    end
end

local function checkout(sha)
    local cfile = OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha
    if not file_exists(cfile) then
        fatal("Commit not found: "..sha)
    end
    local cdata = {}
    for line in io.lines(cfile) do
        local k,v = line:match("^(.-):(.+)$")
        if k and v then
            cdata[k] = v
        end
    end
    local treefile = OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..cdata["tree"]
    if not file_exists(treefile) then
        fatal("Tree object missing for commit "..sha)
    end
    -- restore files
    for line in io.lines(treefile) do
        local fname, fsha = line:match("^(.-):(.+)$")
        if fname and fsha then
            local objf = OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..fsha
            if file_exists(objf) then
                local inf = io.open(objf, "rb")
                local outf = io.open(fname, "wb")
                outf:write(inf:read("*all"))
                inf:close() outf:close()
                print("Restored "..fname)
            end
        end
    end
    local headf = io.open(HEAD_FILE, "w") headf:write(sha) headf:close()
    print("Checked out commit "..sha)
end

local function usage()
    print("minigit <command> [<args>]")
    print("Commands:")
    print("  init                Initialize repository")
    print("  add <file> [...]    Stage file(s)")
    print("  commit <msg>        Commit staged files")
    print("  status              Show staged files")
    print("  log                 Show commit log")
    print("  checkout <hash>     Restore files from commit")
    os.exit(1)
end

-- Main
local cmd = arg[1]
if not cmd then usage() end

if cmd == "init" then
    init()
elseif cmd == "add" then
    if not arg[2] then usage() end
    add({table.unpack(arg,2)})
elseif cmd == "commit" then
    if not arg[2] then usage() end
    local msg = ""
    for i=2,#arg do
        msg = msg .. arg[i] .. " "
    end
    commit(msg)
elseif cmd == "status" then
    status()
elseif cmd == "log" then
    log_()
elseif cmd == "checkout" then
    if not arg[2] then usage() end
    checkout(arg[2])
else
    usage()
end
```

---

## Additional Notes

- **Dependencies:** This code requires Lua's [LuaFileSystem (lfs)](https://keplerproject.github.io/luafilesystem/) module.
  - Install with `luarocks install luafilesystem`
- **SHA1 Calculation:** Uses `sha1sum` — standard on GNU systems. If running on Windows, you may need to change those lines for a compatible hashing command.
- **Executable:** Ensure the file is executable:
  ```sh
  chmod +x minigit
  ```
- **Test:** Run with your test script:
  ```sh
  bash test-v1.sh
  ```

---

If you need adaptation for non-standard behaviors described in your `SPEC-v1.txt`, please provide the file’s content!  
If you have questions or a special requirement for a function, let me know!

#!/usr/bin/env lua

-- Simple MiniGit implementation in Lua
-- Supports: init, add, commit, status, log, checkout

local lfs = require("lfs")

local HEAD_FILE = MINIGIT_DIR .. "/HEAD"
local LOG_FILE = MINIGIT_DIR .. "/log"

    f = io.open(HEAD_FILE, "w") f:close()
    f = io.open(LOG_FILE, "w") f:close()

end

        index[line] = true

    return index
end

    local line = h:read("*l") h:close()
    os.remove(tmpfile)

    return line:match("^(%w+)")
end

local function add(filenames)
    local index = read_index()

        if not file_exists(fname) then
            fatal("File does not exist: "..fname)
        end

end

end

local function commit(msg)

    end

        local sha1 = sha1_file(fname)

            -- store the blob
            local out = io.open(OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha1, "wb")
            local inF = io.open(fname, "rb")
            out:write(inF:read("*all"))
            out:close()
            inF:close()
        end
    end
    -- Store tree object (simple JSON-ish format)
    local tree_obj = ""
    for fname, sha1 in pairs(tree) do
        tree_obj = tree_obj..fname..":"..sha1.."\n"
    end
    local tree_sha1 = sha1_file(OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha1_file(tostring(os.time())))
    local treefile = OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..tree_sha1
    local tf = io.open(treefile, "w")
    tf:write(tree_obj)
    tf:close()

    -- New commit object
    local parent = read_head() or ""
    local commit_obj = {
        tree = tree_sha1,
        parent = parent,
        msg = msg,
        time = tostring(os.time())
    }

    local commit_data = ""
    for k,v in pairs(commit_obj) do
        commit_data = commit_data..k..":"..v.."\n"
    end
    local commit_sha1 = sha1_file(OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..sha1_file(tostring(os.time())..msg))
    local commitfile = OBJECTS_DIR.."/"..commit_sha1

    print("  status              Show staged files")
    print("  log                 Show commit log")
    print("  checkout <hash>     Restore files from commit")

if not cmd then usage() end

if cmd == "init" then
    init()
elseif cmd == "add" then
    if not arg[2] then usage() end
    add({table.unpack(arg,2)})
elseif cmd == "commit" then
    if not arg[2] then usage() end
    local msg = ""
    for i=2,#arg do
        msg = msg .. arg[i] .. " "
    end
    commit(msg)
elseif cmd == "status" then
    status()
elseif cmd == "log" then
    log_()
elseif cmd == "checkout" then
    if not arg[2] then usage() end
    checkout(arg[2])
else
    usage()
end
